ai.bg.title: AI Search Task
ai.confirm.overwriteDraft: You have unsaved draft filters. Overwrite them with the AI search results?
ai.desc: Describe what you're looking for in plain language, and AI will configure the search filters for you.
ai.error.notSupported: Chrome Built-in AI is not supported. Please configure a Custom Cloud AI provider under 'Configure AI...' to enable search.
ai.error.serviceNotInit: AI Service Layer is not initialized.
ai.error.unsupportedDevice: Built-in AI is unsupported on this device. Ensure your system meets hardware requirements (at least 22GB free space) and flags are enabled.
ai.help.example1: active bugs assigned to me created in the last 2 weeks
ai.help.example2: resolved user stories in Sprint 15 or 16
ai.help.example3: tasks for Alex and me with high priority
ai.help.intro: Describe your request in natural language. AI will resolve dates, paths, fields, and assignees.
ai.help.multilingual: Multilingual: Write in English, Russian, French, etc. Roster names are mapped automatically.
ai.helpTooltip.cloud:
Custom Cloud AI

This feature calls your configured cloud AI endpoint (Google Gemini or OpenAI).

External Requests: Your queries, dynamic fields, and work item metadata will be sent to the configured provider API to compile the search filters.
ai.helpTooltip.nano:
Privacy-First On-Device AI

This feature runs entirely locally on your machine using Google Chrome's built-in Gemini Nano model.

100% Private & Secure: Your queries, dynamic fields, and work item data never leave your browser and are never sent to external servers.
ai.info.cloudNeedsConfig: Custom Cloud provider requires configuration. Click 'Configure AI...' to input your API Key.
ai.info.nanoReady: Built-in Gemini Nano model is ready to download. Searching will download the model first (~22MB download, ~250MB unpacked).
ai.inputPlaceholder: e.g., active bugs assigned to me created in the last week...
ai.reasoning.autoTitle: Automatically chooses the best depth based on query complexity
ai.reasoning.balancedTitle: 2-pass schema-filtered. Recommended balance of speed and quality
ai.reasoning.fastTitle: Fast 1-pass response, best for simple queries
ai.reasoning.thoroughTitle: 3-pass reasoning with self-correction. Best for complex logic

help.autoRefresh:
Auto-Refresh
Set the interval for automatic background updates from Azure DevOps, or turn it off.
help.notifyAge:
Notification Age
Define the maximum age threshold (e.g. 2 days) to filter out older historic changes and prevent excessive notifications.
help.telemetry:
Usage Analytics
Send anonymous usage stats (which views/features are used) to help improve the extension. No work-item data, credentials, or personal info is ever sent.
help.theme:
Theme Switcher
Cycle between Light, Dark, or System Sync theme options.
help.timezone:
Timezone Config
Configure the timezone used for active-time calculations and work-hours constraints.
help.uiScale:
UI Scaling
Adjust the overall font and interface scaling factor for high-resolution displays.
help.workHours:
Work Hours
Define your active working hours window used for calculating business seconds and timespans.

setup.footer: ADO Atlas is an independent project — not affiliated with or endorsed by Microsoft. Your token and data stay on this device. Privacy
setup.oauth.copy.title: Copy to clipboard
setup.oauth.intro: Sign in with your Microsoft (Entra ID) account — no token to copy, and it refreshes automatically. A one-time app registration is required; see the setup guide.
setup.oauth.oneClick.title: Sign in with Microsoft in one click — no Entra app registration required (Pro)
setup.oauth.redirectLabel: Redirect URI (register this exact value in your Entra app)
setup.oauth.warning: Work/school (Microsoft Entra) accounts only. Personal Microsoft accounts (outlook/hotmail/live), including a personal account that owns an Azure DevOps org, cannot use OAuth — Azure DevOps has no OAuth resource for them. Use the Token tab (PAT) instead; it works with any account.
setup.pat.expiryLabel: PAT expiry (optional — copy from the token page for a validity countdown)
setup.pat.intro: Talk to Azure DevOps with a Personal Access Token. Create one at dev.azure.com → User settings → Personal access tokens with at least Work Items (Read & Write) and Project and Team (Read) scopes. The PAT is stored in chrome.storage.local on this machine only.
setup.pat.label: Personal Access Token
setup.pat.loadHint: Paste a PAT, then fill in your Organization and Project (both are in your dev.azure.com/<org>/<project> URL). The project list fills in automatically once the org is set.
setup.title: Connect to Azure DevOps
